response.justification: Wei Chen is a skilled polyglot engineer with strong TypeScript/Node.js foundations and genuine software craftsmanship values, but the role demands deep Java and Kotlin expertise, JVM internals knowledge, and hands-on experience with the Spring ecosystem — none of which appear meaningfully in this resume. Java is listed as a skill and appears briefly at Altom (robotic arms) and National Instruments (a C-based IDE, not JVM tooling), but these are peripheral mentions rather than career-defining experience. Kotlin, Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, and enterprise build tooling are entirely absent. The IDE internals experience at NI LabWindows/CVI is the closest signal, but it is C/C++-based and over a decade old, making it insufficient for a mid-senior JVM IDE role.
strengths: Demonstrated IDE-adjacent experience writing components for NI LabWindows/CVI, a developer tooling product
Strong software craftsmanship discipline (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, refactoring dojos) that would translate well to a quality-focused team
Broad systems experience across embedded, high-throughput microservices, and developer tooling shows adaptability and technical depth in non-JVM domains
concerns: No Kotlin experience present anywhere in the resume — a listed required qualification
Zero evidence of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any JVM enterprise framework experience
Core career is built on TypeScript/Node.js and DLang; Java appears only in peripheral, short-tenure roles with no JVM internals focus

key_factors.factor: No Spring or enterprise framework experience
IDE tooling exposure at NI LabWindows
